mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 55% accuracy over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with > 58% accuracy, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during late-cycle expansions, or the signal's predictive power reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annual growth rate surpasses 3.0%, anticipated monetary tightening and margin compression force negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 3.0%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for Fed policy expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening that boosts equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases fails to predict positive 20-day returns with > 55% accuracy, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during periods of high capacity utilization signal supply bottlenecks that disrupt earnings forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns when capacity utilization is above 80%, or the signal shows no predictive edge over a random baseline in walk-forward tests.
